title: China's strategic petroleum reserve as a geoeconomic tool
summary: Chinese policy around the use of their SPR as a geoeconomic tool, particularly in crisis scenarios, is not well understood. This project investigates potential ways for China to do so, but not whether China actually intends to use it in this way.

title: Managed disorder: The hard road to a modest peace with Russia after Ukraine
summary: When the fighting in Ukraine stops, America and Europe will likely end up facing Russia in a low-grade rivalry for years to come. Here is how to prevent that disordered rivalry from sliding into another war

title: Arizona primaries set the stage for contested fall races in a swing state
summary: Arizona Democrats choose who can try to flip a Republican-held House seat and Republicans pick their nominee to try to unseat a Democratic governor.

title: New Air Force One going out of commission to get safety upgrades
summary: The new Air Force One is going out of commission to receive safety upgrades. What needs to be done and was it safe for President Trump to fly the plane to Turkey earlier this month?

title: How Jackson became Democrats’ pick to take on Collins in Maine
summary: Former Maine Senate President Troy Jackson (D) is shifting his attention to a coming battle against incumbent Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine) after pulling off a stunning rise to become the likely Democratic nominee for Senate. Jackson appears poised to formally win the nod Saturday at the party’s nominating convention in Bangor after securing an overwhelming majority of delegates and his main competitors dropped out...

title: GOP faces real danger in voter enthusiasm gap
summary: It’s halfway through the summer, and despite the midterm curse that has hammered the party with the White House in all but three midterms since the Civil War, Republicans are looking a lot better than expected. The best evidence: The generic ballot is pretty well tied, even though the incumbent president is underwater with voters....

title: GOP faces new alarm over Iran as ceasefire implodes
summary: The intensifying military conflict with Iran and its impact on gas prices is sparking fresh alarm among GOP lawmakers, who are urging President Trump to find a way to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible to avoid an angry backlash from voters. Oil prices have soared again since a ceasefire with Iran...

title: What to watch in Arizona’s primaries
summary: The Grand Canyon State is holding an earlier-than-usual July primary on Tuesday, teeing up consequential races for governor, key statewide election offices and congressional races. Arizona lawmakers passed legislation in February to change their primary date from August to July to make sure the state had enough time to prepare for the November general election. Republicans are eyeing a rare opportunity to oust...

title: Mamdani’s threat to arrest Netanyahu spotlights deepening Democratic divisions
summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s threat to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu signals a new line in the sand for the Democratic Party and its relationship to the Jewish State. Mamdani’s comments to the New York Times that he is having “an active conversation” over whether an arrest warrant for Netanyahu can be...

title: Once Anti-Abortion, Now a Progressive: The Evolution of a Maine Democrat
summary: Troy Jackson, who is expected to become his party’s nominee in the state’s marquee Senate race, has long been an economic populist, but his positions on social issues have changed markedly over time.

title: The U.S. Is Pulling Back on Energy Efficiency
summary: The Trump administration is undoing fuel-saving efforts even as war in Iran, data centers and heat waves are pushing up gasoline and electricity costs.

title: The Ordeal of a 42-Day Hantavirus Quarantine: ‘I Felt Like a Prisoner’
summary: Eighteen Americans were isolated for weeks in a Nebraska quarantine facility after being exposed to the deadly virus on a cruise ship. Many found the experience traumatic.

title: Trump Rejected Nearly 6,000 Clemency Applications Amid Hope of Pardon Wave
summary: Expectations were raised for a raft of pardons in connection with the country’s 250th birthday but the president granted fewer than 20 on July 3.

title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

title: Landor v. Louisiana Department of Corrections and Public Safety: Supreme Court Bars Prisoner’s Religious Liberty Suit Against Prison Officials
summary: In Landor v. Louisiana Department of Corrections and Public Safety, the Supreme Court ruled that individuals cannot be sued “in their private capacities” under the Religious Land Use and Institutionalized Persons Act (RLUIPA), which generally prohibits prisons that take federal funding from substantially burdening prisoners’ religious exercise, if the individuals do not “voluntarily and knowingly” agree to comply...

title: Living with Wet Basements
summary: This report presents findings from a study conducted in Pittsburgh to document the prevalence of and experiences with basement wetness, the barriers that residents face in addressing it, and options available to solve the problem.

title: Examining the Impact of Senate Bill 1160 on Utilization Review and Medical Treatment in California Workers' Compensation
summary: Senate Bill 1160 reduced utilization review (UR) requirements for some treatments during the first 30 days after a work-related injury. The authors examined effects on UR processes and medical treatment in California’s workers’ compensation system.

title: Securing AI Algorithmic Insights
summary: In this report, the authors extend a security framework to provide the first systematic roadmap for protecting algorithmic insights, the novel techniques, methods, and design know-how that materially improve artificial intelligence systems.

title: Restricting AI Agent Use of Biological Tools
summary: AI agents can help non-experts use advanced biological design tools, raising misuse concerns. The authors explore safeguards built into biological tools that could block such assistance and find that their effectiveness is currently limited.

title: Another Hormuz? What to Know About the Houthi Threat to the Red Sea
summary: The Yemen-based Houthis’ decision to impose a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia opens a new front in the U.S.-Iran war and threatens to choke off the Red Sea’s Bab el-Mandeb Strait, with potentially major consequences for the global economy.

summary: Hoover Institution fellow Glenn Tiffert testifies in front of the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform about winning the economic competition with China in all areas including working families, the AI race, and energy.

title: JD Vance’s Ambiguous Message On Christianity and America
summary: Although generally neglected, mocked, or reviled by scholars and progressive intellectuals, religion shapes and colors American politics. When it recedes from hearts and minds, traditional faith leaves a spiritual vacuum into which flow all manner of superstitions, false prophets, and utopian delusions.

title: Self-Regulation Meets The Open-Weight Problem
summary: AI self-governance is coming just like we recommended, but the release of the most-powerful open-weight Chinese AI model yet raises huge questions about it. This and more in this week's System Check.

title: The Research Driving The Billionaire Tax Act Debate
summary: A Hoover Institution paper, authored by Hoover fellows Benjamin Jaros, Joshua Rauh, Gregory Kearney, et al., disputes the $100 billion claim and estimates revenues at about $40 billion, with a range between $35 billion and $46 billion, and a theoretical floor much lower.

title: With just a week left until August recess, Congress faces long to-do list
summary: Before the House adjourns for its August recess, House Speaker Mike Johnson wants to advance more defense funding, the president's election integrity bill and a stock trading ban for lawmakers.

title: Colombia's Petro calls on supporters to defend economic reforms in last presidential speech
summary: Outgoing Colombian President Gustavo Petro called on his supporters to protect his government's social and economic reforms and resist any efforts by the nation's incoming administration to change labor laws.

title: Deadly shootings by ICE officers renew scrutiny of recruitment and training
summary: Two deadly shootings involving ICE officers reignited protests and renewed questions about tactics, training and recruitment at the agency. None of the agents involved were wearing body cameras, and neither of the victims was the intended target of the operation. Amna Nawaz discussed more with John Sandweg, who served as acting director of ICE under President Obama.

title: Netanyahu government defies court, raising constitutional crisis fears as election nears
summary: The Israeli parliament has dissolved, paving the way for October elections. The country's longest-serving leader is bracing for one of his toughest reelection fights. Benjamin Netanyahu faces national questioning over his leadership during and since the Oct. 7 attacks, including a constitutional crisis pitting his government against the judiciary. Producer Karl Bostic reports with Nick Schifrin.

title: Tamara Keith and Jasmine Wright on the political fallout of Trump's address

summary: State lawmakers are limiting the special advantages that let teachers’ unions lobby on the public dime. Teachers’ unions have long enjoyed access to public payroll systems and facilities that no private entity receives. Recent legislation in three states demonstrates a better path: public funds are redirected toward improving student outcomes, not strengthening union infrastructure. Idaho got the ball rolling on...

summary: America has much to celebrate this July 4, but also much to do. Even after 250 years, the task of conserving the Republic given to us by our Founding Fathers is a never-ending struggle. title: Small Business Size Representations and Protests
